glossary.def: The visual analogue of a word embedding: one fixed-size square patch of an image, flattened and linearly projected into the language model's embedding space as a single d-dimensional vector. An H x W image at patch size P yields N = HW/P^2 patch tokens; the transformer then attends to them exactly as it attends to word tokens.

The single learned linear map W (shape d x P^2C) that turns a flattened image patch into one model-width token, plus an added learned positional embedding. Shared across all patches, it is equivalent to a stride-P convolution and is the only new module LLaVA adds on top of a frozen vision encoder and a frozen LLM.

A transformer whose context window holds both image tokens and text tokens, generating text autoregressively over the combined sequence. Most modern VLMs reuse a pre-trained (usually CLIP) vision encoder and a pre-trained language model, joined by a small projection or by cross-attention layers.

Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training: two encoders (image and text) trained on hundreds of millions of image-caption pairs to make a true image-caption pair score higher (cosine similarity) than all mismatched pairs in the batch. Its image encoder produces language-aligned visual features and is the standard vision front end for downstream VLMs.

Concatenating projected image tokens with text tokens into one sequence so the LLM's existing self-attention mixes both, in every layer. Maximally interactive and minimal to implement (the LLaVA recipe), but the image tokens consume context-window slots and the self-attention cost grows as (N+T)^2.

Keeping the text sequence as the only main sequence and letting it attend into a separate image memory through dedicated cross-attention layers inserted between the LLM's blocks (the Flamingo recipe). Queries come from text, keys and values from the N image features, costing N*T and leaving the base LLM's weights and context length untouched.

The second training stage of LLaVA-style VLMs: fine-tuning the projection and usually the LLM on multimodal instruction-following data (image + question -> answer, visual chat, OCR, grounding), with cross-entropy over the answer tokens only. LLaVA bootstrapped this data by prompting a strong text LLM with image annotations to author the conversations.

A VLM confidently describing objects that are not present in the image, the failure mode that benchmark accuracy tends to hide because the cross-entropy objective rewards fluent, plausible answers without grounding them to actual pixels. Adversarial probes such as POPE exist specifically to measure it.

refs.cite: Radford, A. et al. (2021). Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP). ICML 2021.
Alayrac, J.-B. et al. (2022). Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning. NeurIPS 2022.
Liu, H., Li, C., Wu, Q. & Lee, Y. J. (2023). Visual Instruction Tuning (LLaVA). NeurIPS 2023.
Dosovitskiy, A. et al. (2021). An Image Is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale (ViT). ICLR 2021.
Li, J., Li, D., Savarese, S. & Hoi, S. (2023). BLIP-2: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training with Frozen Image Encoders and LLMs. ICML 2023.
Li, Y. et al. (2023). Evaluating Object Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models (POPE). EMNLP 2023.
Yue, X. et al. (2024). MMMU: A Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark. CVPR 2024.
